element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns population grows based on service availability and conditions
Player learns economic balance between residential demand and commercial viability
Player learns pollution and crime mechanics; industrial placement affects residential desirability
Player must balance power plant placement against pollution; blackouts cause population decline
Traffic management affects city health; player learns road/rail placement strategy
Player learns service coverage directly influences residential satisfaction
Player learns tax rates affect population happiness and city budget sustainability
Random or scenario-triggered; player learns reactive crisis management
Catastrophic events requiring rapid infrastructure rebuilding
Can trigger secondary disasters (train crashes); tests city resilience

typical_tension_triggers: Population emigration due to poor conditions
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Long-cycle tension where player actions have delayed consequences; disaster events create acute stress peaks; no arcade pressure, all tension is strategic/systemic

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Blank map allows organic city growth; players signal intent through zoning patterns

difficulty_curve.early_game: Very permissive sandbox mode introduces mechanics gradually; player learns residential growth precedes commercial/industrial needs
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Infrastructure complexity increases; player must balance multiple competing systems (power, transport, zoning, services, pollution)
difficulty_curve.late_game: Economic management becomes primary challenge; large cities require careful fiscal planning to avoid bankruptcy
notable_spikes: Large city population threshold (demands more infrastructure coordination)
Scenario modes with time limits and specific win conditions (traditional game pressure contrasts sandbox)

notable_level_patterns.description: No goals, no time limit, player-driven pacing. Learning happens through experimentation and failure. Disasters occur randomly.
Time-limited, goal-oriented maps based on real or fictional cities. Player must achieve objectives before deadline. Win/loss conditions create traditional game pressure.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: All scenario maps (modeled after real cities with specific goals)

notable_level_patterns.description: Scenario-triggered catastrophes test player's reactive crisis management and rebuild capability
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Scenarios featuring tornadoes, monster attacks, fires, earthquakes
